Find the @belt/tools package:
1. Search for belt/tools directory or package
2. Look for createSchema, createType, createSlice, createMachine exports
3. Find usage examples if any exist
4. Check if it's a local workspace package or external

Return the API surface and how to use it correctly.

{
  "name": "@mms/system",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "format": "prettier --write",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate",
    "db:push": "drizzle-kit push",
    "db:studio": "drizzle-kit studio",
    "test": "bun test src/",
    "test:unit": "bun test src/ --grep -v 'integration'",
    "test:integration": "bun test src/**/*.integration.test.ts",
    "test:e2e": "bunx playwright test",
    "test:e2e:ui": "bunx playwright test --ui",
    "test:e2e:debug": "bunx playwright test --debug",
    "test:e2e:report": "bunx playwright show-report",
    "test:all": "bun test && bun run test:integration && bun run test:e2e",
    "demo:generate": "bun run src/demo/cli.ts",
    "demo:seed": "bun run src/demo/cli.ts --trains 128 --seed 20260121 --output drizzle/manual/seed_demo.sql"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@belt/tools": "0.1.2",
    "@blueprintjs/core": "6.1.0",
    "@blueprintjs/icons": "6.1.0",
    "@deck.gl/aggregation-layers": "9.2.5",
    "@deck.gl/core": "9.2.5",
    "@deck.gl/layers": "9.2.5",
    "@deck.gl/react": "9.2.5",
    "@hono/zod-openapi": "1.2.0",
    "@opentelemetry/api": "^1.9.0",
    "@opentelemetry/sdk-node": "^0.57.0",
    "@opentelemetry/sdk-trace-base": "^1.30.0",
    "@opentelemetry/semantic-conventions": "^1.28.0",
    "@types/pdfmake": "0.2.12",
    "bullmq": "^5.34.8",
    "chokidar": "5.0.0",
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "date-fns-tz": "^3.2.0",
    "drizzle-orm": "^0.44.0",
    "exceljs": "4.4.0",
    "hono": "4.11.4",
    "ioredis": "^5.4.2",
    "konva": "10.2.0",
    "papaparse": "5.5.3",
    "pdfmake": "0.3.3",
    "pino": "^9.6.0",
    "postgres": "^3.4.5",
    "prom-client": "^15.1.3",
    "react": "18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "18.3.1",
    "react-konva": "18.2.14",
    "uuid": "13.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.1",
    "@playwright/test": "1.57.0",
    "@types/bun": "1.3.6",
    "@types/chokidar": "2.1.7",
    "@types/node": "^24.10.1",
    "@types/papaparse": "5.5.2",
    "@types/react": "18.3.12",
    "@types/react-dom": "18.3.1",
    "@types/three": "0.170.0",
    "@types/uuid": "11.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^5.1.1",
    "drizzle-kit": "^0.30.5",
    "eslint": "^9.39.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^7.0.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "^0.4.24",
    "globals": "^16.5.0",
    "typescript": "~5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.46.4",
    "vite": "^7.2.4"
  }
}

file.content:
{
  "name": "@belt/tools",
  "description": "TypeScript utility library with runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers",
  "type": "module",
  "sideEffects": false,
  "version": "0.1.2",
  "author": "Antonio Presto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
  "license": "MIT",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt",
    "directory": "packages/tools"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt/tree/main/packages/tools#readme",
  "keywords": [
    "typescript",
    "validation",
    "state-management",
    "schema",
    "runtime-types",
    "helpers",
    "utilities"
  ],
  "main": "./dist/index.cjs",
  "module": "./dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/index.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/index.cjs"
    },
    "./schema": {
      "types": "./dist/schema.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/schema.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/schema.cjs"
    },
    "./state-machine": {
      "types": "./dist/state-machine.d.ts",
      "import": "./dist/state-machine.mjs",
      "require": "./dist/state-machine.cjs"
    }
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite build --watch",
    "test": "vitest run --passWithNoTests",
    "test:watch": "vitest --watch --passWithNoTests",
    "coverage": "vitest --coverage",
    "check": "run-s check:*",
    "check:types": "tsc --noEmit",
    "check:lint": "eslint src --fix",
    "check:format": "prettier --write ."
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "immer": "^11.1.3",
    "lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "lru-cache": "^11.2.4",
    "minimatch": "^10.1.1",
    "typebox": "^1.0.73"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@belt/prettier-plugin": "0.0.987",
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.2",
    "@types/lodash": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/node": "^25.0.3",
    "@types/react": "^19.2.7",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react-swc": "^4.2.2",
    "eslint": "^9.39.2",
    "npm-run-all": "^4.1.5",
    "prettier": "^3.7.4",
    "prettier-plugin-organize-imports": "^4.3.0",
    "rollup-plugin-node-externals": "^8.1.2",
    "terser": "^5.44.1",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3",
    "typescript-eslint": "^8.52.0",
    "vite": "^7.3.0",
    "vite-plugin-dts": "^4.5.4",
    "vite-plugin-externalize-deps": "^0.10.0",
    "vite-tsconfig-paths": "^6.0.3",
    "vitest": "^4.0.16"
  }
}

content.text: Let me read the README and type definitions to understand the API surface:

content.content:
1→# @belt/tools 2→ 3→A TypeScript utility library providing runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers. 4→ 5→## Installation 6→ 7→```bash 8→bun add @belt/tools 9→``` 10→ 11→**Peer Dependencies:** 12→ 13→- `react` >= 18.0.0 (optional, only for state-machine hooks) 14→ 15→## Features 16→ 17→- **Schema Validation** - Runtime type validation with TypeBox 18→- **State Machine** - Immer-based state management with React hooks 19→- **Helpers** - Common utilities (date, string, object, etc.) 20→- **Type Utilities** - Advanced TypeScript type helpers 21→ 22→--- 23→ 24→## Quick Start 25→ 26→### Schema Validation 27→ 28→```tsx 29→import { createType, createSchema, isType, InferType } from '@belt/tools'; 30→ 31→// Create a validated model 32→const UserModel = createSchema({ 33→ id: 'number', 34→ name: 'string', 35→ email: 'string?', // optional 36→ roles: 'string[]', // array 37→ createdAt: 'date', // Date object 38→}); 39→ 40→type User = InferType; 41→ 42→// Parse and validate data 43→const user = UserModel.parse({ 44→ id: 1, 45→ name: 'John', 46→ roles: ['admin'], 47→ createdAt: new Date(), 48→}); 49→ 50→// Validation check 51→if (UserModel.isValid(someData)) { 52→ // TypeScript knows someData is User 53→} 54→ 55→// Complex nested types 56→const OrderType = createType({ 57→ properties: { 58→ customer: { 59→ properties: { 60→ name: 'string', 61→ address: { 62→ properties: { 63→ city: 'string', 64→ zip: 'string', 65→ }, 66→ }, 67→ }, 68→ }, 69→ items: { 70→ items: { 71→ properties: { 72→ sku: 'string', 73→ quantity: 'number', 74→ }, 75→ }, 76→ }, 77→ total: 'number', 78→ }, 79→}); 80→ 81→// Type guard 82→if (isType(someValue, 'number')) { 83→ console.log(someValue.toFixed(2)); // TypeScript knows it's a number 84→} 85→``` 86→ 87→### State Machine 88→ 89→```tsx 90→import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@belt/tools'; 91→ 92→// Sync slice for local state 93→const counterSlice = createSlice({ 94→ id: 'counter', 95→ initial: { 96→ count: 0, 97→ lastUpdated: null as Date | null, 98→ }, 99→ cache: { policy: 'session' }, 100→ reducers: { 101→ increment: state => { 102→ state.count += 1; 103→ state.lastUpdated = new Date(); 104→ }, 105→ decrement: state => { 106→ state.count -= 1; 107→ state.lastUpdated = new Date(); 108→ }, 109→ reset: state => { 110→ state.count = 0; 111→ state.lastUpdated = null; 112→ }, 113→ }, 114→}); 115→ 116→// Async slice for data fetching 117→const usersSlice = createAsyncSlice({ 118→ id: 'users', 119→ cache: { 120→ policy: 'public', 121→ ttl: '5m', 122→ swr: true, 123→ }, 124→ context: {}, 125→ load: async () => { 126→ const response = await fetch('/api/users'); 127→ return response.json(); 128→ }, 129→}); 130→ 131→// Create the machine 132→const appMachine = createMachine({ 133→ id: 'app', 134→ slices: { 135→ counter: counterSlice, 136→ users: usersSlice, 137→ }, 138→ context: { userID: null }, 139→}); 140→ 141→// Use in React components 142→function Counter() { 143→ const { state, actions } = appMachine.useMachine('counter'); 144→ 145→ return ( 146→
147→ Count: {state.count} 148→ 149→ 150→
151→ ); 152→} 153→``` 154→ 155→### Helpers 156→ 157→```tsx 158→import { 159→ getProperty, 160→ setProperty, 161→ ms, 162→ formatDate, 163→ invariant, 164→ tryCatch, 165→ keyBy, 166→ compose, 167→} from '@belt/tools'; 168→ 169→// Deep property access (type-safe) 170→const street = getProperty(user, 'address.street'); 171→ 172→// Time conversion 173→const timeout = ms('5m'); // 300000 (5 minutes in ms) 174→const debounce = ms('300ms'); // 300 175→const ttl = ms('1h'); // 3600000 (1 hour) 176→ 177→// Date formatting (uses date-fns) 178→const formatted = formatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); 179→ 180→// Assertions 181→invariant(user.id, 'User ID is required'); 182→ 183→// Safe error handling 184→const [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 185→ 186→// Array to object by key 187→const usersById = keyBy(users, 'id'); 188→ 189→// Function composition 190→const processData = compose(JSON.stringify, addMetadata, validate); 191→``` 192→ 193→--- 194→ 195→## API Reference 196→ 197→### Schema Module 198→ 199→| Function | Description | 200→| ---------------------------------- | ------------------------------------- | 201→| `createType(definition)` | Create a runtime validation type | 202→| `createSchema(properties)` | Shorthand for object type definitions | 203→| `isType(value, definition)` | Type guard for validation | 204→| `invariantType(definition, value)` | Assert value matches type | 205→ 206→**Type Definition Syntax:** 207→ 208→- Primitives: `'string'`, `'number'`, `'boolean'`, `'date'`, `'null'`, `'undefined'` 209→- Optional: `'string?'`, `'number?'` 210→- Arrays: `'string[]'`, `'number[]'` 211→- Objects: `{ properties: { ... } }` 212→- Enums: `{ enum: ['a', 'b', 'c'] }` 213→- Unions: `{ union: ['string', 'number'] }` 214→- Records: `{ record: 'string' }` 215→ 216→### State Machine Module 217→ 218→| Function | Description | 219→| -------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | 220→| `createMachine(config)` | Create a state machine instance | 221→| `createSlice(config)` | Create a synchronous slice | 222→| `createAsyncSlice(config)` | Create an async slice for data fetching | 223→ 224→**Cache Policies:** 225→ 226→- `public` - Shared cache, TTL-based invalidation 227→- `private` - User-specific cache, invalidated on user change 228→- `session` - Session-specific cache, invalidated on app restart 229→ 230→**Machine Options:** 231→ 232→- `trackMetadata` - Enable state change tracking (default: `false`) 233→ 234→### Helpers Module 235→ 236→| Function | Description | 237→| ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | 238→| `getProperty(obj, path)` | Type-safe deep property access | 239→| `setProperty(obj, path, value)` | Type-safe deep property setter | 240→| `ms(timeString)` | Convert time string to milliseconds | 241→| `formatDate(date, format)` | Format date using date-fns | 242→| `invariant(condition, message)` | Assert condition or throw | 243→| `tryCatch(fn)` | Safe function execution | 244→| `compose(...fns)` | Right-to-left function composition | 245→| `keyBy(array, key)` | Convert array to object by key | 246→| `maskData(value)` | Mask sensitive data for logging | 247→ 248→### Type Utilities 249→ 250→```tsx 251→import type { 252→ Writable, // Remove readonly modifiers 253→ Compute, // Flatten intersection types 254→ Paths, // All dot-notation paths of object 255→ PathType, // Type at specific path 256→ Falsy, // Union of falsy values 257→ InferType, // Infer type from schema definition 258→} from '@belt/tools'; 259→``` 260→ 261→--- 262→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

     3→ * Checks if value matches type

import { InferType, TypeDefinition, TypeDefinitionString } from './infer-type';
/**
 * Checks if value matches type
 * @param value
 * @param definition
 *
 * @example
 *  isType(new Date('BANANA'), 'date') // => false; value is an invalid date;
 *  isType(1, 'number') // => true; value is a number
 *  isType(undefined, 'number?') // => true; value matches type `number?`
 *  isType(undefined, 'number') // => false; value should be a number
 */
export declare function isType<Type extends TypeDefinitionString>(value: unknown, definition: Type): value is InferType<Type>;
/**
 * Asserts that value is of type `Type`
 * @param value
 * @param definition
 * @param parentFunction
 * @param customMessage
 *
 *  @example
 *  const x = ['banana'] as unknown;
 *  assertType('string[]', x)
 *  console.log(x) // typescript now infers x as string[]
 *
 *  @example:
 *  // will throw 'Expected value to match type "string[]"; found an invalid "Number" instead.',
 *  assertType('string[]', 1)
 */
export declare function invariantType<Type extends TypeDefinition>(definition: Type, value: unknown, parentFunction?: Function, 
/**
 * Custom message
 * Accepts replacements `$typename`, `$originalMessage` and `$definition`.
 */
customMessage?: string): asserts value is InferType<Type>;
